steps.title: Locate the reason for the difference
steps.content: The passage gives a speed difference (389 vs. 80 km/hr) and then provides the reason: flight features limit speed less than running features. The blank must express what CAUSES this difference.

steps.title: Test each choice for causation
steps.content:
• Choice A — a consequence: The speed difference is a RESULT of how flight and running features differ in limiting speed. This correctly captures cause-and-effect. ✓
  • Choice B — an objective: This would mean the speed difference is a GOAL of the structural features, not something that results from them. The passage describes an outcome, not an intended aim. ✗
  • Choice C — a repudiation: This would mean the speed difference REJECTS or contradicts the structural features, which is illogical in context. ✗
  • Choice D — an explanation: This would mean the speed difference EXPLAINS the structural features, reversing the cause-and-effect direction the sentence requires. ✗
A is correct.
